content: Hi, I'm Skyler. I'm a podcast host, and I've been really busy lately with work and school.
Hi Skyler, nice to meet you. Congratulations on the podcast and the school work. What's your podcast about, and what are you studying?

Thanks! My podcast is about true crime, and I'm really passionate about exploring the stories and psychology behind the cases. I'm also studying for my part-time MBA, which has been a challenge but really rewarding.

True crime is so fascinating, and it's great that you're pursuing your passion. An MBA is a big undertaking, but it's wonderful that you're committed to your education. What inspired you to start your podcast, and what do you love most about it?

I've just always been interested in the psychology and sociology of crime, and I felt like a podcast was a great way to explore those topics and share my knowledge with others. I love the research and storytelling aspects of it, and it's been really rewarding to connect with listeners and hear their feedback.
I'm actually based in Salt Lake City, UT, and I love the outdoor recreation opportunities here. In my free time, I enjoy hiking and tennis, and I'm also really into calligraphy.

Salt Lake City is a beautiful city, and it's great that you have access to so many outdoor activities. Hiking and tennis are great ways to stay active, and calligraphy is such a unique and creative hobby. What do you love most about calligraphy, and do you have any favorite scripts or styles?

Actually, I love the elegance and precision of calligraphy. I've been practicing with a few different scripts, including copperplate and brush script, and I'm really enjoying the process of learning and improving.

That's wonderful. Calligraphy is such a beautiful art form, and it's great that you're dedicated to learning and mastering it. Do you have any favorite books or podcasts on true crime, or any recommendations for listeners?
Yeah, I actually love the book 'I'll Be Gone in the Dark' by Michelle McNamara. It's a really well-researched and compelling story, and it's been a big inspiration for my own podcast.
That's a great book. Michelle McNamara's work is really exceptional, and it's wonderful that you're inspired by her writing. Do you have any plans for upcoming episodes or seasons of your podcast?

Actually, I'm planning to release a new season in a few months, and I'm really excited about the cases and topics we'll be covering. I'm also hoping to collaborate with some other true crime podcasters and experts in the field.

That sounds like a great plan. Collaborations can be really rewarding, and it's wonderful that you're looking to connect with other podcasters and experts. Do you have any favorite true crime cases or stories that you're looking forward to covering in the future?
Yeah, I'm actually really interested in the Ted Bundy case, and I'm planning to do a deep dive into his story and psychology in an upcoming episode.
The Ted Bundy case is so fascinating and disturbing. It's a really complex and thought-provoking topic, and I'm sure you'll do a great job exploring it in your podcast.

Thanks! I'm really looking forward to it. I've also been thinking about starting a Patreon or membership program for my podcast, to help support the production costs and allow listeners to get more exclusive content.

That's a great idea. Patreon and membership programs can be really effective ways to support creators and connect with fans. I'm sure your listeners will be happy to support your work and get more exclusive content.
Yeah, I'm really hoping it will help me take my podcast to the next level and allow me to produce even more high-quality content for my listeners.
I'm sure it will. You're doing great work, and it's wonderful that you're dedicated to your passion and your audience.
